title: Zillow Q1 Results Improve, Cuts Q2 Outlook
text: Zillow Q1 2024 Earnings and Q2 Guidance. Q1 2024 Results: Zillow exceeded expectations on key metrics. Revenue: $529 million (vs. $508.63 million analyst estimate) — a 13% year-over-year increase. Adjusted EBITDA: $125 million (vs. $104 million prior year) — representing 20.2% growth. Net Loss: $23 million, compared to $22 million in Q1 2023. Segment performance showed mixed results with residential revenue up 9% to $393 million, while rentals surged 31% to $97 million. Q2 2024 Guidance: The company provided a more cautious outlook for the second quarter, forecasting revenue between $525-540 million. This guidance fell short of analyst expectations at $559 million. Market Reaction: The reduced forward guidance prompted investor concern. The stock fell as much as 8% and ended the day down 4.8% following the announcement. This disappointment reflected broader concerns about the cooling housing market's impact on the company's near-term performance.
